published_prior_art.cite: Puterman1994 and Altman1999: standard finite-state average-reward/occupation-measure support formula

repo_prior_art.cite: nearest are pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2, pid_long_term_surrogate_v1, and pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1; none states a reversible-spell Cesaro endpoint-diameter convergence frontier

published_prior_art.cite: Han2021 finite dynamic sequence identification; ChernozhukovFernandezValHahnNewey2013/JunLeeShin2016 imposed panel homogeneity or stationarity; none subsumes the finite-window iff frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: nearest repo proposals do not give a reversible-spell block-switching endpoint-cycling witness

published_prior_art.cite: Schneider1993/RockafellarWets1998 cover generic compact-set/support instability; causal finite sharpness construction is not published but the targeted workflow claim is not established

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The claimed no-free-stationary-summary warning names KalouptsidiKitamuraLimaSouzaRodrigues2020 and DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 as finite sharp-bounds workflows, but neither is shown to publish the extrapolation being refuted; as written tier=letter below novelty_target=flagship.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The long-run dynamic-treatment comparison misses van den Berg and Vikstrom 2022, Econometrica, 'Long-Run Effects of Dynamically Assigned Treatments,' a closer long-run dynamic-treatment benchmark even though it does not subsume the partial-ID frontier.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Retarget Conjecture 2 to a specific published estimator, software workflow, or applied reporting convention that actually replaces horizon-by-horizon dynamic bounds by one stationary interval, with a quoted claim and bibkey; otherwise demote it to an illustrative sanity example and let Conjecture 1 carry the flagship claim.

Add a Related work paragraph comparing Conjecture 1 to van den Berg and Vikstrom 2022 and, if relevant, transition-effect bounds such as Vikstrom/Ridder-style long-run partial-ID results, making clear that those handle dynamic assignment/duration selection rather than reversible-spell sharp-set convergence.

State the flagship axis explicitly: either frame Conjecture 1 as a new sharp long-run partial-ID regime-opening result, or add a computable threshold separating equivalence/non-equivalence between two named published long-run workflows.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison and either give Conjecture 2 a real published target or demote it so the proposal can meet the flagship floor through Conjecture 1.
